title: Mbappé scores two goals and leads France to World Cup semifinals after defeating Morocco
summary: The French forward adds eight goals in the tournament and ties Messi, as the team prepares for the next round.

title: Reform of the Imperial House Law passes to the Lower House with support from four parties
summary: The proposal includes the study of succession to the throne and faces opposition from the Constitutional Democratic Party in the Upper House.

title: Japan accelerates development of specialized artificial intelligence in 19 sectors and humanoid robots
summary: The government promotes AI in areas such as pharmaceuticals and law enforcement, while private companies make multi-million dollar investments.

title: Global tension: Putin and Zelensky compete for Trump's favor as pressures on Iran increase
summary: The former US president emerges as a key player in the conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East, conditioning his military and tariff support.

title: Japan's wholesale price index rises 7.1% year-on-year in June, exceeding forecasts
summary: The biggest increase in decades, driven by higher oil prices, pressures the Bank of Japan to consider monetary adjustment.

title: Bankruptcy of payment processor Zentoshin leaves 63 financial institutions with bad loans
summary: The Japanese government sets up a consultation center for SMEs affected by the collapse of the card payment company.

title: Japanese ships cross the Strait of Hormuz as secret negotiations on Iran's nuclear program continue
summary: Despite exchanges of attacks, the United States and Iran hold parallel talks to reach an agreement on atomic development.

title: Tokyo Stock Exchange rises more than 1,000 points driven by AI and semiconductor boom
summary: The Nikkei index recovers to 68,700 yen, infected by Wall Street's tech optimism and the success of SK Hynix's IPO.

title: Wave of traffic accidents in Japan leaves several dead and arrested for reckless driving
summary: A fatal hit-and-run of a student in Osaka, a collision at 135 km/h, and the spread of dangerous driving videos mark the day.

title: Refereeing controversy at World Cup: English player sent off and growing criticism of VAR system
summary: FIFA faces questions about the consistency of its refereeing decisions, as complaints about video refereeing intensify among teams.

title: Debate in Japan over bill against flag desecration and shareholder pressure on media
summary: The government promotes penalizing the burning of the national flag, while public media face demands for 8% profitability from their investors.

title: Typhoon 9 approaches the Sakishima Islands in Okinawa with strong winds and risk of flight cancellations
summary: The storm strengthens the Pacific high-pressure system, causing sweltering heat in central and western Japan.

title: Fifteen schoolchildren injured after being attacked by a swarm of wasps in Ibaraki
summary: The students, including middle schoolers, are taken to hospital after wasps attacked them in a park in Tsukuba.

title: Japanese politics shaken: Reiwa Party changes leader and Centrist faces internal tensions
summary: Yamamoto Tarō leaves politics and his party changes name, while the merger of the Centrist with the Constitutional Democratic stagnates due to discrepancies over security and nuclear energy.

title: Bank of Japan maintains regional economic assessment while government underscores its autonomy
summary: The monetary authority preserves its judgment on all regions of the country backed by the AI and semiconductor sector, while the growth minister avoids interfering in interest rates.

title: Assembly begins of the main float of the Gion Festival in Kyoto, the iconic Naginata Hoko
summary: The famous summer festival of the ancient imperial capital begins preparations for its main procession that will run through the streets of Kyoto's historic center.

title: Extreme heat wave and tragic drowning of two teenagers in a river in Shiga Prefecture
summary: Authorities issue heat stroke alerts in inland regions as water accidents multiply during the summer.

title: Chinese stock markets fall despite ninth consecutive month of price increases and yuan appreciation
summary: The Shanghai index retreats dragged down by financial stocks, countering the strength of the local currency set by the Chinese central bank.

title: Yakult achieves a combined no-hitter while Rōki Sasaki shows notable improvement
summary: Three foreign pitchers for the Tokyo Swallows make history in the Central League, while the young talent of the Chiba Marines recovers his velocity in six innings.

title: Historic trial in Osaka against prosecutor accused of abuse of authority and the emblematic case of 'contempt of prosecution'
summary: The court examines allegations of mistreatment of a subordinate in the Special Prosecution Office of Osaka, in the context of the trial against the former prosecutor.
